file_purpose: Complete Project Understanding with 15-Step Development Process

steps.step_title: Project Description & Objectives
steps.achieved: Full project description collated from 3 source files (landing HTML, app v3 HTML, TechSpec v1 docx) and user requirements message
steps.last_position: Step 1 completed. All source material analysed and synthesized.
steps.next_step: Proceed to Step 2 - Divide into 15-20 logical modules

content.description: ComplianceTrack is a universal compliance tracking platform designed for the AI era. It enables organisations of all sizes, across all industries and geographies, to manage every type of compliance (IT, tax, legal, regulatory, operational, environmental) from a single portal. The platform is built around the principle that missed compliance deadlines are never a knowledge problem but always a coordination problem. By providing one unified view of every deadline, every owner, and every change, it eliminates the gap between departments that leads to penalties.
target_audience: Chartered Accountants (CAs) managing multiple client organisations
IT personnel managing software licence and cybersecurity compliance
CFOs / Finance Controllers tracking GST, TDS, advance tax deadlines
HR Managers handling PF, ESIC, POSH, labour law compliance
Company Secretaries managing MCA filings, DIR-3 KYC, board meetings
Operations Heads managing factory licences, pollution control, FSSAI
Import/Export managers handling IEC, DGFT, FEMA compliance
Organisations of all sizes from sole proprietors to large enterprises
Any business worldwide needing deadline and obligation tracking
core_objectives: Provide a single source of truth for all compliance obligations across all departments
Enable unique URL/access per compliance tracker with role-based visibility (view/edit/no access)
Capture every activity with full audit trail including timestamp, user identity, IP address, and machine ID
Support AI-era interactions: direct human use, human-via-AI, AI-autonomous, email (human and AI)
Enable connectivity via API, access tokens, and MCP (Model Context Protocol) for third-party integrations
Provide pendency tracking across 8 time buckets: Delayed, 24h, 7d, 30d, 60d, 90d, 180d, 365d
Support multiple sales and distribution channels with commission, discount, and incentive tracking
Allow document attachment via email, Google Drive, and in-app uploads with full versioning
Enable data submission and retrieval from external portals via user-granted API/MCP tokens
Maintain complete field-level change history with strikethrough visibility

channel_1_freelance_agents.name: Freelance Human Agents
channel_1_freelance_agents.description: Individual agents who get unique access links to their portal. Can view commissions earned, discount codes assigned to them, and incentive milestones.
channel_1_freelance_agents.discount_mechanism: Central discount portal pushes country-specific discount codes to agents of that country.
channel_1_freelance_agents.incentive_structure: Milestone-based, e.g., 'Have 2 paid customers, earn X extra'

channel_2_ai_agents.name: AI Agent Sales (Claude, etc.)
channel_2_ai_agents.description: AI agents like Claude and various other AI systems will sell the platform directly to users through conversational interfaces.

channel_3_company_agents.name: Company-Created Agent Networks
channel_3_company_agents.description: Companies can create their own human agent networks to sell ComplianceTrack under their brand.

channel_4_digital_marketing.name: Digital Advertising & Email Campaigns
channel_4_digital_marketing.description: Google Ads, social media advertising, and targeted email marketing campaigns.

channel_5_tele_sales.name: Telesales (AI or Human)
channel_5_tele_sales.description: Outbound telesales through both AI-powered calling systems and human sales representatives.

channel_6_firms.name: Accountant Firms & Audit Firms
channel_6_firms.description: Direct B2B sales through accounting and audit firm partnerships.

channel_7_individuals.name: Individual Accountants & Auditors
channel_7_individuals.description: Individual practitioners who recommend or resell the platform to their clients.

channel_8_enterprise.name: Enterprise / Government Partnerships
channel_8_enterprise.description: Partnerships with enterprises like BSNL Enterprise for distribution to their existing customer base.

multi_client.name: For CA Firms & Audit Practices - Multi-Client

account_admin.description: Full control over the account. Can appoint multiple account admins. Can manage all users, all departments, all compliances, all settings.

client_department_admin.description: Can manage within their assigned client/department scope. Can appoint client/department admins, editors, and viewers within their scope.

editor.description: Can add, edit, update compliances within their assigned scope. Changes are logged with full audit trail.

viewer.description: Read-only access to compliances within their assigned scope. Can see audit logs and history.

unique_url_system.description: Each compliance tracker has a unique URL. Clicking it lands on the tracker. Access is determined by the user's rights: if they have rights they can view/edit; if not, they must request access from the account admin.

steps.step_title: Module Division - 18 Logical Modules
steps.achieved: Divided the entire system into 18 logical, independently deployable modules based on source documents and user requirements
steps.last_position: Step 2 completed. 18 modules defined with dependencies.
steps.next_step: Proceed to Step 3 - Describe each module in detail with database entities

modules.description: Foundation layer. All tables, RLS policies, indexes, migrations. Every other module depends on this.
Passcode-based auth, magic links, session tokens, IP/machine ID capture, access request workflow.
Organisation-level data isolation, org creation for firms vs clients, cross-org visibility for CAs.
4-tier rights: Account Admin, Client/Department Admin, Editor, Viewer. Per-compliance, per-department, per-account scoping.
Core compliance CRUD, unique URLs, status lifecycle (draft/published/completed/overdue), owner assignment, reassignment, compliance library.
Internal audit findings, remediation tracking, linkage to parent compliances.
Department management, regional grouping, organisation hierarchy.
User invitation, access request/approval, team member management, role assignment.
Immutable audit log capturing every action with timestamp, user ID, IP address, machine ID. Field-level change tracking with strikethrough.
Document upload, versioning, email attachment parsing, Google Drive integration, proof of compliance storage.
8-bucket pendency reminders, daily digest email, WhatsApp alerts, in-app notifications, escalation rules.
Claude AI integration, natural language compliance parsing, PDF/document auto-fill, AI-generated compliance library, cost tracking.
Public REST API, access token management, MCP (Model Context Protocol) support, webhook system, AI-detection middleware.
Freelance agent portal, commission tracking, discount code management (country-specific), incentive milestones, company agent networks.
Full desktop web application: dashboard, compliance table, detail panel, admin panel, audit log viewer, daily digest preview, AI chat interface.
Responsive mobile web / native app: simplified dashboard, quick compliance view, push notifications, mobile document capture.
Email-based compliance updates, document submission via email, email parsing for compliance data, AI email processing.
CI/CD pipeline, environment configuration, Vercel deployment, Supabase migrations, monitoring, cost limits, logging.

steps.step_title: Module Descriptions with Database Entities
steps.achieved: Detailed description of all 18 modules with their database entities, key features, and technical specifications
steps.last_position: Step 3 completed. All modules described with DB entities.
steps.next_step: Proceed to Step 4 - Write detailed JSON for Module 1 (Database Schema)

modules.purpose: Foundation data layer for the entire platform. Defines all tables, relationships, RLS policies, and migration strategy. Built first; all other modules import from here.

database_entities.description: One row per customer/tenant. Root of all data segregation.
One row per human user. Belongs to exactly one organisation.
Active user sessions with device/IP tracking.
Requests from people who arrive without invitation.
Core compliance records. Each has a unique URL slug.
Field-level change history. Every edit creates a row.
Immutable log of every system action.
Track AI API costs per organisation.

design_principles: Every table has: id (UUID), org_id (UUID FK), created_at, updated_at, created_by (UUID FK to users)
org_id is the tenant key. All queries MUST include org_id in WHERE clause
RLS policies enforce org_id automatically at database level
Soft deletes only (deleted_at timestamp) - data is never physically removed
All timestamps in UTC. Display layer converts to user timezone
UUID v4 for all primary keys - never sequential integers
JSONB for flexible metadata fields - version controlled via history tables

modules.purpose: Handle user authentication via passcode (no passwords), magic link email login, session management with IP/machine ID capture, and access request workflows for uninvited users.
key_features: Passcode-based login (4-6 digit, hashed with salt)
IP address and machine ID capture on every session
Access request workflow for uninvited users

modules.purpose: Ensure complete data isolation between organisations. For CA firms, each client is a separate org with separate user records. The CA firm manages clients from a parent dashboard.
key_features: Row Level Security (RLS) at PostgreSQL level
Parent-child org relationships for CA firms
Country and timezone settings per org
Per-client portal provisioning for multi-client plans

modules.purpose: Implement 4-tier access rights system with per-compliance, per-department, and per-account scoping. Account Admins can appoint other admins and manage all roles.
key_features: 4 roles: Account Admin, Client/Dept Admin, Editor, Viewer
Account Admin can appoint multiple Account Admins
Client/Dept Admin can appoint within their scope
Permission change logging in audit trail

modules.purpose: Core compliance CRUD operations, lifecycle management (draft to completed), unique URL generation, owner assignment and reassignment, and compliance library functionality.
key_features: CRUD operations with full audit trail
Status lifecycle: draft > published > in_progress > completed / overdue
Unique URL per compliance for direct access
Load from library function for onboarding
Priority levels: low, medium, high, critical

modules.purpose: Manage internal audit findings, remediation actions, and their linkage to parent compliance items.
key_features: Audit point creation linked to compliances

modules.purpose: Manage departments within organisations, regional groupings, and hierarchical organisation structure.
key_features: Default departments: Finance, IT, HR, Legal, Operations, Import/Export

modules.purpose: Manage user invitations, access requests and approvals, team member listing, and role assignment within the organisation.
key_features: Access request submission and admin approval
Multi-org user support (separate records per org)

modules.purpose: Immutable audit logging of every system action with full context including timestamp, user identity, IP address, and machine ID. Supports field-level change tracking.
key_features: IP address and machine ID on every log entry
Filterable and searchable audit log viewer
Compliance-specific change history with strikethrough view

modules.purpose: Handle document uploads, versioning, email attachment parsing, Google Drive integration, and compliance proof storage.
key_features: Google Drive integration for document linking
File type validation and size limits

modules.purpose: 8-bucket pendency tracking and reminders, daily digest emails, WhatsApp alerts, in-app notifications, and escalation rules.
key_features: Daily digest email with compliance summary
Escalation rules (notify manager if overdue > X days)

modules.purpose: Claude AI integration for natural language compliance parsing, PDF/document auto-fill, compliance library generation, and AI-driven insights.
key_features: Natural language to compliance form: POST /api/v1/ai/parse-text
AI-generated compliance library for new organisations
AI chat interface for compliance queries
Cost tracking per organisation with daily limits
AI usage logging and billing data
AI detection middleware for API calls

modules.purpose: Public REST API with access tokens, MCP (Model Context Protocol) support for AI tool connections, webhook system, and AI-detection middleware.
key_features: MCP endpoint for AI model connections
API request logging in audit trail
AI detection: identify if request is from AI vs human
Data submission/retrieval from external portals via user-granted tokens

modules.purpose: Manage all 8 sales channels, freelance agent portal, commission tracking, discount code management, incentive milestones, and company agent networks.
key_features: Country-specific discount code management from central portal
Incentive milestone tracking (e.g., 2 paid customers = X bonus)

modules.purpose: Full desktop web application with dashboard, compliance table, detail panel, admin panel, audit log viewer, daily digest preview, and AI chat interface.
modules.database_entities: Uses all tables via API layer
key_features: Slide-out detail panel (Details, Comments, History, Documents tabs)

modules.purpose: Mobile-responsive web app and/or native app with simplified views for on-the-go compliance management.
modules.database_entities: Uses all tables via API layer

modules.purpose: Enable compliance updates, document submissions, and data extraction via email. Support both human and AI email interactions.
key_features: AI email processing for data extraction

modules.purpose: CI/CD pipeline, environment configuration, deployment automation, monitoring, cost management, and operational tooling.
key_features: GitHub Actions CI/CD: lint > test > build > deploy
Production deployment on merge to main

steps.step_title: Detailed JSON - M01: Database Schema & Core Data Layer

design_principles: Every table: id (UUID PK), org_id (UUID FK), created_at, updated_at, created_by (UUID FK)
org_id is tenant key. All queries MUST filter by org_id
RLS policies enforce org_id at DB level - not application level
Soft deletes only (deleted_at timestamp)
All timestamps UTC. Display layer converts to timezone
UUID v4 for all PKs. Never sequential integers
JSONB for flexible metadata. Version controlled via history tables

tables.name: organisations
tables.description: One row per customer/tenant. Root of all data segregation.

columns.name: parent_org_id
columns.description: For multi-client: CA firm org is parent

tables.rls_policy: Users can only read their own org and child orgs (if parent)

tables.name: users
tables.description: One row per human user. Belongs to exactly one org. Same email can exist in different orgs.
tables.rls_policy: Users can only see other users in same org

tables.name: sessions
tables.description: Active sessions with device/IP tracking for audit.

tables.name: access_requests
tables.description: Uninvited users requesting access. Admins approve/reject.

tables.name: compliances
tables.description: Core compliance records. Unique URL slug per compliance.

columns.name: external_references
columns.description: Links to external portals, filing references

tables.name: compliance_history
tables.description: Field-level change history. Every edit creates a row for each changed field.

columns.name: compliance_id
columns.constraints: NOT NULL FK -> compliances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

columns.name: compliance_id
columns.constraints: NOT NULL FK -> compliances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE

tables.name: audit_log
tables.description: Immutable log of every system action. Never edited or deleted.
tables.rls_policy: No RLS. Audit log is append-only. Users see their org's logs.

tables.name: departments
tables.description: Departments within an organisation. Supports hierarchy.

tables.name: ai_usage
tables.description: Track AI API costs per organisation for billing and limits.

content.migration_strategy: Supabase CLI. Versioned migration files. Never modify past migrations - only add new ones.

steps.step_title: Detailed JSON - M02: Authentication & Session Management
steps.achieved: Complete auth specification including passcode login, magic links, session management, IP/machine capture, and access requests
steps.next_step: Proceed to Step 6 - Detailed JSON for M03: Multi-Tenancy & Data Segregation

api_endpoints.description: Send 6-digit passcode to email. Creates or finds user.
logic: Generate 6-digit code, hash with salt, store hash
Rate limit: 1 per minute per email
Check user is_active and not deleted
Create session with IP, machine_id, user_agent
Log login in audit_log with IP and machine ID

api_endpoints.description: Send magic link to email for passwordless login.
logic: On click: verify token, create session, redirect to dashboard

api_endpoints.description: Submit access request for uninvited users.
logic: Check if user already exists in org
Notify all account_admins of the org via in-app + email

api_endpoints.description: Validate current session and return user info.

security_measures: Rate limiting: 1 passcode per minute, 5 attempts per 15 minutes
JWT with 24-hour expiry, refresh token with 7-day expiry
IP address captured on every auth event
Machine ID captured using browser fingerprinting

session_management.token_format: JWT with org_id, user_id, role, permissions payload

steps.step_title: Detailed JSON - M03: Multi-Tenancy & Data Segregation
steps.achieved: Complete multi-tenancy specification with RLS policies, org hierarchy, and data isolation
steps.next_step: Proceed to Step 7 - Detailed JSON for M04: RBAC

isolation_strategy.method: Shared database with Row Level Security (RLS)
isolation_strategy.enforcement_level: Database level (PostgreSQL RLS policies), NOT application level
isolation_strategy.description: Even a compromised API endpoint cannot read another tenant's data because the database itself enforces isolation.

multi_client_ca_firm.description: CA firm is parent org. Each client is a child org with parent_org_id pointing to the CA firm.
multi_client_ca_firm.data_visibility: CA firm Account Admins can view (but not edit) child org compliance dashboards. Each child org is fully isolated from other child orgs.
multi_client_ca_firm.user_isolation: CA firm users exist only in parent org. Client users exist in child orgs. No user record is shared.
multi_client_ca_firm.pricing: Base INR 30,000 for parent org + INR 3,000 per child org (one-time each).

company_agent_network.description: Company creates parent org. Company's agents sell to end users. Each end user org is a child.
company_agent_network.data_visibility: Company can see aggregate stats of child orgs but not individual compliance data.

rls_policies.description: Audit log has NO with_check because it is INSERT-only from service key

api_endpoints.description: Create new organisation (for onboarding or CA adding client).
api_endpoints.auth: Account Admin only. Or system onboarding.

api_endpoints.description: List child organisations (for CA firms / company networks).
api_endpoints.auth: Account Admin of parent org only

api_endpoints.description: Add a new client org under current org (multi-client plans).
api_endpoints.auth: Account Admin of parent org. Plan must be multi_client.

tenant_context_middleware.description: Every API request must resolve the tenant context before any data access.
flow: 2. Decode JWT to get org_id and user_id
3. Set PostgreSQL search_path or RLS variable: SET app.current_org_id = '{org_id}'
4. All subsequent queries automatically filtered by RLS

steps.step_title: Detailed JSON - M04: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)
steps.achieved: Complete RBAC with 4 roles, permission scoping, per-entity access, and API endpoints
steps.next_step: Proceed to Step 8 - Detailed JSON for M05: Compliance Management Engine

roles.description: Full control over the account. Can do everything within the organisation.
Can manage within their assigned departments. Can appoint within scope.
roles.scope: Limited to assigned departments and their compliances

roles.description: Can add and edit compliances within their assigned scope.
roles.scope: Limited to explicitly assigned compliances and departments
Limited to explicitly granted compliances and departments

permission_scoping.description: Permissions are scoped at three levels: Account (org-wide), Department, and Individual Compliance. A user with 'viewer' role may be granted edit access to a specific compliance or department via explicit permission grants.

api_endpoints.description: Invite a user to the organisation.
logic: Create user record with invited role
Log permission change in audit_log with old/new value

api_endpoints.description: Grant scoped permission (e.g., editor access to specific compliance).
Approve or reject an access request.
logic: If approved: create user record, send welcome email

permission_check_flow: 1. Decode JWT to get user_id, org_id, role
2. For the requested action, check role permissions matrix
3. If action requires scoped permission, check permission_scopes table
4. If user has account_admin role: allow all
5. If user has dept_admin role: check if entity belongs to their departments
6. If user has editor/viewer role: check permission_scopes for explicit grant
7. If no permission found: return 403 Forbidden
8. Log access attempt in audit_log if denied

steps.step_title: Detailed JSON - M05: Compliance Management Engine
steps.achieved: Complete compliance engine with CRUD, lifecycle, unique URLs, owner management, library, and recurrence
steps.next_step: Proceed to Step 9 - Detailed JSON for M06: Audit Points

transitions.trigger: Cron job when due_date passes and status is published
Cron job when due_date passes and status is in_progress
Owner marks done after due date

access_control.has_rights: Show compliance details with edit/view based on role
access_control.no_rights: Show access request form. User must request access from account admin.
access_control.not_logged_in: Redirect to login page, then return to this URL after auth

unique_url_system.slug_generation: Title slugified + 6-char random suffix for uniqueness

api_endpoints.description: List compliances with filters, sorting, pagination.

api_endpoints.auth: editor+ (within scope) or account_admin or dept_admin
logic: If status=published: set owner_id, create notification to owner

api_endpoints.description: Get compliance detail with full data.

logic: Compare old and new values for each field
Create compliance_history row for each changed field (old_value, new_value, IP, machine_id)
If status changed: create notification, log in audit_log
If owner changed: notify new owner, notify old owner

api_endpoints.description: Reassign owner of one or multiple compliances.
api_endpoints.auth: account_admin or dept_admin or current owner

api_endpoints.description: Load compliance library templates for the organisation.
logic: Call AI engine to generate compliance templates

api_endpoints.description: Get pendency counts across all 8 buckets.

cron_jobs.name: overdue_checker
cron_jobs.logic: SELECT compliances WHERE status IN ('published','in_progress') AND due_date < CURRENT_DATE AND deleted_at IS NULL. Update status to 'overdue'. Create notification. Log in audit_log.

cron_jobs.name: recurrence-generator
cron_jobs.logic: For completed compliances with recurrence_rule, create next occurrence with new due_date based on rule.

cron_jobs.name: pendency-bucket-refresher
cron_jobs.logic: Refresh materialized view or cache of pendency counts per org.

logic: Check if user already exists in org
If exists and active: return error
If new: create user record with is_active=true
Send invitation email with magic link
If role changed: log in audit_log with old/new role

api_endpoints.description: Get current user profile with permissions.

team_collaboration_features: Share compliance view-only snapshot via WhatsApp/Email
Daily digest email to all relevant users

steps.step_title: Detailed JSON - M09: Activity Logging & Audit Trail
steps.achieved: Complete audit trail with immutable logging, field-level history, strikethrough view, IP/machine capture
steps.next_step: Proceed to Step 13 - Detailed JSON for M10: Document Management

design_principles: IMMUTABLE: audit_log rows are NEVER updated or deleted
APPEND-ONLY: only INSERT operations allowed
COMPREHENSIVE: every create, update, delete, status change, login, export, API call logged
FIELD-LEVEL: compliance_history tracks individual field changes with old/new values
IDENTITY: every log includes user_id (or AI marker), IP address, machine ID
TIMESTAMPED: all logs have created_at in UTC

logging_middleware.description: Automatic logging for all API endpoints
logging_middleware.implementation: Next.js middleware that wraps all /api/ routes

field_level_history.trigger: Automatic on every PATCH to compliances table
field_level_history.implementation: PostgreSQL trigger function that compares NEW and OLD row

strikethrough_display.description: In the UI, old values are shown with strikethrough styling and new values are highlighted. This gives a visual 'track changes' effect.

api_endpoints.description: List audit log entries with filters.
Get field-level change history for a compliance.
Export audit log as CSV or Excel.

ai_action_detection.description: The system must identify when an action is performed by AI vs human.
detection_methods: MCP tool calls include ai_model in metadata
ai_action_detection.logging: When AI detected: set is_ai_action=true and ai_model field in audit_log

columns.name: parent_doc_id
columns.description: For versioning: points to previous version

supabase_storage.description: Primary storage. Files stored in Supabase Storage buckets.

google_drive.description: Link documents from user's Google Drive. Store reference, not file.

email_attachment.processing: Parse email, extract attachments, store in Supabase Storage

api_endpoints.description: Link a Google Drive file to a compliance.

steps.step_title: Detailed JSON - M11: Notifications, Reminders & Daily Digest
steps.achieved: Complete notification system with 8 pendency buckets, daily digest, WhatsApp, escalation
steps.next_step: Proceed to Step 15 - Detailed JSON for M12: AI Engine

pendency_buckets.sql: due_date < CURRENT_DATE AND status NOT IN ('completed','archived')
due_date BETWEEN CURRENT_TIMESTAMP AND CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '24 hours' AND status NOT IN ('completed','archived','overdue')
due_date BETWEEN CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '24 hours' AND CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '7 days' AND status NOT IN ('completed','archived','overdue')
due_date BETWEEN CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '7 days' AND CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '30 days' AND status NOT IN ('completed','archived')
due_date BETWEEN CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '30 days' AND CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '60 days' AND status NOT IN ('completed','archived')
due_date BETWEEN CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '60 days' AND CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '90 days' AND status NOT IN ('completed','archived')
due_date BETWEEN CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '90 days' AND CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '180 days' AND status NOT IN ('completed','archived')
due_date BETWEEN CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '180 days' AND CURRENT_TIMESTAMP + INTERVAL '365 days' AND status NOT IN ('completed','archived')

daily_digest.schedule: Every day at 8:00 AM organisation timezone
daily_digest.recipients: All active users in the organisation

content_structure.ai_summary: AI-generated 2-3 sentence summary of overall compliance health

sections.title: Overdue (Action Required)
sections.items: All delayed compliances with owner name, days overdue, title

daily_digest.delivery: SendGrid email. Optionally WhatsApp if enabled for org.

escalation_rules.action: Send urgent WhatsApp + email to all admins
Flag as critical in daily digest with red highlight

escalation_rules.condition: No owner assigned and due within 7 days

api_endpoints.description: Get unread notification count for badge.
Preview today's daily digest without sending.

cron_jobs.name: daily-digest
cron_jobs.description: Generate and send daily digest for each org

cron_jobs.name: reminder-24h
cron_jobs.description: Send reminders for compliances due within 24 hours

ai_detection_for_incoming_requests.description: When the system receives API requests, it must identify if they are from AI agents.
methods: API tokens created with is_ai_agent=true flag
MCP connections inherently identified as AI
ai_detection_for_incoming_requests.handling: AI-identified requests get is_ai_action=true in audit_log. AI agents can be rate-limited differently. AI agents can bypass certain UI-only checks but get stricter audit logging.

new_tables.name: api_tokens

external_data_submission.description: Users can grant API/MCP access tokens to submit data to or retrieve data from external portals (government filing portals, bank portals, etc.)
external_data_submission.flow: User provides their access_token/api_key for external portal -> System stores encrypted -> System can make API calls on behalf of user -> Results stored as compliance data or documents

List of customers acquired by this agent.
Create discount code from central portal.

inbound_email_processing.description: Parse incoming emails to extract compliance updates and documents.
flow: Receive email via SendGrid Inbound Parse or webhook
Extract sender email, match to user in org
Parse subject/body for compliance references (compliance ID, title)
If AI enabled: send to Claude for structured data extraction
Extract attachments: store as documents linked to compliance
Create comment on compliance with email content summary

tech_stack.monitoring: Vercel Analytics + Supabase Dashboard + custom error tracking

ci_cd_pipeline: GitHub Actions: lint > unit tests > integration tests > build
On PR to main: preview deployment on Vercel with test Supabase
On merge to main: production deployment to Vercel + Supabase migrations
M18_infrastructure.deployment_principle: Each module (M01-M17) independently deployable. Database (M01) deployed first, never modified without migration.

for_zai_or_claude: 1. Read this JSON file from top to bottom. It contains the complete project specification.
2. Check the 'steps_completed' array to know which steps are done.
3. Each step has: timestamp, status, achieved (what was done), last_position (where we stopped), next_step (what to do next).
4. To continue work: find the first step with status != 'completed' and follow its next_step instruction.
5. After completing work on a step: update status to 'completed', update achieved and last_position, set next_step, and update timestamp.
6. Save the JSON file after every change.
7. Database tables are specified in Steps 4 (M01) and individual module steps. All tables include org_id for tenant isolation.
8. API endpoints follow REST convention: /api/v1/{resource}. All require Authorization: Bearer JWT or Bearer API token.
9. Every data mutation MUST be logged in audit_log with IP address and machine ID.
10. The system must detect AI agents (via header, token flag, or MCP endpoint) and tag audit_log entries accordingly.
11. Unique URLs: each compliance has /{org_slug}/c/{compliance_slug}. Access is controlled by RBAC + permission_scopes.
12. Pendency tracking: 8 time buckets calculated from due_date. Used in dashboard, notifications, and daily digest.
13. Sales channels: 8 different channels. Agent portal with commissions, country-specific discount codes, incentive milestones.
14. Touchpoints: 10 different ways to interact (human laptop, human mobile, AI laptop, AI mobile, AI direct, human email, AI email, docs via email, Google Drive, MCP/API).
15. The landing page (compliancetrack_landing.html) shows the marketing site. The app (compliance_tracker_v3.html) shows the working prototype UI.

critical_dependencies.M01 must be built first: All other modules depend on database tables
critical_dependencies.M03 must be built third: All data access requires tenant context
critical_dependencies.M05 is the core feature: Most UI screens and notifications depend on compliance data

testing_requirements: Unit tests for all API endpoints

source_files_analyzed: compliancetrack_landing.html - Marketing landing page with features, pricing, BSNL partnership
compliance_tracker_v3.html - Working app prototype with dashboard, compliance table, detail panel, admin panel, audit log
ComplianceTrack_TechSpec_v1.docx - 24-module technical specification with DB schema, API endpoints, infrastructure
